title: How to make a Reap Card repayment via bank transfer
body: What are repayments? Learn more here about repayments and the different repayment methods. In this article, you will learn how you can repay your Reap card statement by making a fiat bank transfer, without any additional fees. Step-by-step guide Navigate to Reap Card > Repayment and click Make Repayment In the repayment flow, select "Bank transfer" You will then see the bank account information to make your repayment (you can find all the banking details at the end of this document): for Hong Kong Dollars (HKD) accounts, you will see the relevant FPS info for United States Dollars (USD) accounts, you will see bank account information Then process to make the bank transfer from your bank. Pro-tip: Please make sure to include the reference ID that is provided in the comments/details of your transfer. This will help us process your repayment and attribute the funds way faster. Once you have completed the transfer, click on the "I have transferred" button to create a notification for our team to begin the reconciliation process. Pro-tip: You may upload the payment proof in the following popup to help us spot the funds faster. Please make sure to press done after inputting all details. Done! Banking details United States Dollars accounts (USD) SWIFT Code: DHBKHKHH Bank Name: DBS Bank (Hong Kong) Limited Bank Address: 18th Floor, The Center, 99 Queen’s Road Central, Central, Hong Kong HK Bank Code: 016 Branch Code: 478 Bank Account Number: 000921993 Beneficiary Name: Reap Technologies Limited Intermediary Bank: JP Morgan Chase Bank, New York, USA SWIFT: CHASUS33 If your banking partner is in Hong Kong, feel free to make a local USD transfer! Hong Kong Dollars accounts (HKD) FPS ID: 102163383 Beneficiary Name: Reap Technologies Limited Bank Name: DBS Bank (Hong Kong) Limited Bank Address: 18th Floor, The Center, 99 Queen’s Road Central, Central, Hong Kong HK Branch Code: 016 Branch code: 478 Account number: 001786374 FAQ How fast can I expect my repayment to be reconciled? Our Finance Team will reconcile the repayment within 1-3 working days. You will receive a notification once the repayment is completed, and the repayment status will be updated as "completed" on your dashboard. How can I speed up the reconciliation process? Including the Reference ID in your transfer details and attaching a proof of transfer during the flow will significantly increase the processing time down to almost T+0! What happens if I send the wrong currency? If you have sent a currency different from the ones your account is in, please let us know at support@reapglobal.co in order to ensure an efficient process. My repayment is not reconciled yet, what should I do? If you didn't see the repayments reconciled in time, please email support@reapglobal.co with a proof of transfer and the following information so that we can follow up with the team appropriately: The name of the entity making the payment The amount being sent The bank name from which the transfer is made How will I know if my repayment has been reconciled? Once a repayment is reconciled, a new line is generated on the Reap Card's Transactions page. You will also receive a notification via email. Who is responsible for the bank handling charges? Please note that any handling charges imposed by the banks are the payer's responsibility and won't be covered by Reap. Can I use USD to settle my HKD Reap Card balance? No, unfortunately, you cannot use USD to settle your HKD Reap Card balance. Please ensure that you are making payments in the correct currency corresponding to your card balance. If your Reap Card balance is in HKD, it should be settled using HKD. Any transactions attempting to use USD to pay an HKD balance will not be successful. Can I make a repayment using a third party provider? To avoid delays, please ensure that the bank account holder is the same as your registered entity in Reap. Reap reserves the right to reject repayments made from unapproved sources. Please note that we strictly do not support third-party repayment.

title: How to Replace Your Reap Card
body: If your Reap Card is lost, stolen, damaged or expiring, you can easily request a replacement using our self-service card replacement process. This ensures minimal disruption and allows you to manage your card replacements without the need for support. Tip: If you identify suspicious activity or temporarily misplace your card, freezing it is the quickest way to prevent unauthorized transactions without permanently disabling the card. You can easily unfreeze it later if everything checks out. You can follow these steps. - How to freeze and unfreeze a card? Some quick points to note before you request your replacement card: For Lost or Stolen Cards If your card is lost or stolen, please replace it as soon as possible to prevent unauthorized transactions. Once you report your card lost or stolen in the dashboard: Your card will be immediately deactivated to prevent fraud. Your new card will be created with a new card number, expiry date, and CVV. Once processed, you will receive an email update. Your new card will be issued, with an estimated delivery time of 7–8 business days for local delivery (Hong Kong) and 10 business days for international delivery. Once activated, your new card is ready for use. If you see any unrecognised transactions, please follow these steps - How to Report Suspected Fraudulent Transactions? For Damaged Cards If your card is damaged but still functional, you can request a new one: Your existing card will remain active until your new card has been received and is activated. Your new card will be created with the same card number but a new expiry date and CVV. Once processed, you will receive an email update. Your new card will be issued, with an estimated delivery time of 7–8 business days for local delivery (Hong Kong) and 10 business days for international delivery. Once activated, your new card is ready for use. For Expiring Cards If your card is expiring, you have 2 options. You can manually request a new card or wait for your replacement card to be automatically issued to you. For a Manual Replacement You will get an email 90 days before your card expires. You can log in and follow the steps below to manually order your replacement card. Your existing card will remain active until your new card has been activated or the card reaches its expiry date. Your new card will be created with the same card number but a new expiry date and CVV. Once processed, you will receive an email update. Your new card will be issued, with an estimated delivery time of 7–8 business days for local delivery (Hong Kong) and 10 business days for international delivery. Once activated, your new card is ready for use. For an Automatic Renewal You will get an email 90 days before your card expires. If you take no action, a replacement card will be issued, to your saved address, 30 days before expiry. Your new card will be created with the same card number but a new expiry date and CVV. Your new card will be automatically linked to your account, once activated, your old card will be deactivated. Your new card will be issued, with an estimated delivery time of 7–8 business days for local delivery (Hong Kong) and 10 business days for international delivery. Once activated, your new card is ready for use. How to request a replacement for a lost, stolen, damaged or expiring card Who Can Replace Cards? Cardholders can replace their own physical cards. Admins and Group Owners can replace cards for their team members. How Do I Order A Replacement Card? Log into your Reap Account Navigate to Reap Card > Card and find the card you want to replace. Click the “…” on the selected card to expand the options. Select Replace Card. Choose a Reason for Replacement Expiring Card – Your card is due to expire soon. Lost or Stolen – Your card has been misplaced or stolen. Damaged Card – Your card is physically damaged and needs a replacement. Confirm Your Shipping Details Review your delivery address to ensure your new card is sent to the correct location. Update the address if necessary before confirming your request. Review & Confirm Double-check all details before submitting your request. Once confirmed, you will see a “Card Ordered” badge in your dashboard.

title: Accounting Integrations FAQ
body: What are the differences between "Sync as Bank Statement" and "Sync as Reconciled"? Which sync option is better for me? This depends on your company’s accounting practice. If you use accrual accounting and prefer to manage the reconciliation process in the accounting software, the ‘sync as a bank statement’ option will be better for you. However, if you would like to simplify the reconciliation process, the ‘sync as reconciled’ option in Reap is better. Having matched all the spending categories in the Reap dashboard before syncing to accounting software will save you the time and effort to re-do the reconciliation. How often does sync occur? To suit your varying accounting needs, you can choose different syncing methods for specific Reap transactions. All transaction data in Reap will not be synced to accounting software without your review and approval. Can I use different methods to sync my transactions to my accounting software? Yes, you can. What is the best way to sync the data? We suggest setting up ‘Groups’ on the Reap dashboard according to the spending tracking categories before the sync.

title: Is Reap safe?
body: Security is the foremost important element of the Reap platform. In this article, we hope to give you clarity on our actions to ensure the safety and security of our user data and the platform. Account and data We use the latest Transport Layer Security to keep your data encrypted during transit. In addition, your login credentials are stored only on your device, never on our server, nor in the cloud. ‍ Reap uses the AES-256 encryption technique, the same used by major banks and governments worldwide, which is what we use to keep your data safe. To safeguard your payments, we apply 2-step verification to prevent any unauthorised transactions. Secure infrastructure Our servers are situated in secure ISO27001 compliant data centres , and we run services from the Amazon Web Service cloud to protect users from all web-based attacks and abuse. ‍Availability is of paramount importance in the cloud. Our servers are designed with an automatic response to any security risks, meaning you do not have to worry about downtime. Reap system has passed multiple security penetration tests so that we eliminate any security vulnerabilities. We periodically conduct these tests even before any external attackers may have the chance to, and consistently upgrade our systems to prevent new threats. Keeping your payments safe Reap is PCI certified , under the Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard - a global standard for ensuring data security followed by banks and card networks. We comply with these requirements for organisations to safely and securely accept, store, process, and transmit cardholder data during credit card transactions to prevent fraud and data breaches. Reap does not store full credit card details nor pass them through any servers. All payments are secured and processed in a PCI certified payment gateway . Any funds processed are safeguarded by multiple layers of security and compliance checks on its way to your recipient.

title: Is it secure to connect my bank account with Reap?
body: With any software that can access sensitive financial data, it is natural for customers to want to know exactly what information is being obtained, how it is being used and what measures are taken to guarantee that the data is kept private and secure. Here’s everything you need to know about how the bank connection works and what measures are taken to ensure your information is kept secure. Why does Reap ask to connect to my bank account? Reap needs to understand your business finances in order to assess and optimise your credit limit for your Reap Card. The traditional way, and the way most banks and virtual banks in Hong Kong still currently use, is to ask you to provide various supporting documents such as recent bank statements and audited financial statements. We know first-hand how time consuming and inconvenient that is. At Reap, we look to streamline and automate all processes, including the secure provision of supporting documents. For this goal we have partnered with Finverse, a technology company founded in Hong Kong, that acts as an intermediary link between your bank account and financial applications like Reap. The technology itself is not new and is already widely used globally across a multitude of banks and applications. How does the bank connection work and is it safe? When you link your bank account you are providing Finverse the permission to sign-into your bank on a one-time basis in order to access your bank account information (such as account balance and transactions). Finverse stores the financial information it retrieves using bank-level AES-256 encryption, which Reap can then access via a secure connection. Finverse later deletes your financial information to ensure it cannot be reused or shared with anyone other than Reap. Reap is never ever able to see, store, or use your login information. Instead, your login information is communicated directly to Finverse, which immediately encrypts the login information and passes it to specialized systems dedicated to logging in to your selected bank. Your login information is decrypted by those systems only for the purpose of logging in, and then is immediately deleted from memory. Finverse never stores your login information (even encrypted) in any database. The bank connections are designed to only retrieve financial information from your accounts. Neither Reap nor Finverse can make transactions on your account or submit instructions on your behalf in any way. What information is viewed and how is it used? Reap only views the financial information that you consent and authorise us to connect to. Primarily that will be a snapshot of your account balance at the time of the connection. We use this information for the sole purpose of assessing your credit limit. Only our credit risk officers are given access to view your information, on a need-to-know basis. In accordance with our respective privacy policies, neither Reap nor Finverse will ever sell or rent your personal information. Okay I understand, but I still do not want to connect, what should I do? If you’d prefer not to connect to your bank account, please select “Upload Bank Documents” and we will strive to process your application as soon as we can. For more information on how Finverse collects, uses, and protects your information, please visit Finverse’s Privacy Policy. For information on how Reap uses your linked bank account, please visit our Terms of Service and Privacy Policy .

title: How to Add Team Members to Different Entities
body: If your organization manages multiple entities in Reap, you can add team members to one or more entities depending on their role and responsibilities. To help keep access secure across entities, new team members must be added using a business email address . Adding a User to Another Entity You can invite an existing team member to join a different business entity, even if they already have a Reap account. For example, if a team member currently belongs to Entity B, and an admin from Entity A wants to invite them, simply: Go to the Team page in the dashboard Select Invite Team Member Enter the user’s business email address Assign the appropriate role and permissions for the new entity Send the invitation The team member will receive an email and a dashboard notification prompting them to accept the invite. Once accepted, they can switch between entities using the multi-entity dropdown in the dashboard. Personal email domains (e.g. Gmail, Yahoo, Outlook) can’t be used to add new team members—even if they already belong to another entity. If Access Isn’t Working as Expected If a team member can’t join another entity: Confirm the invite was sent to their business email address Ask the admin to resend the invitation if necessary Make sure the user is using the original invite email , not a forwarded version For large teams, multi-entity onboarding, or time-sensitive access needs, Reap Support can help review your setup. Need Help? If you need help managing team access across multiple entities, Know who to talk to @ Reap.

title: What is Reap Treasury?
body: Reap Treasury provides an overview of your account assets and fund allocation. This includes the types of digital currencies (USDT/ USDC) you've sent to Reap for various purposes. It also allows for a much faster fund reconciliation whilst sending funds (within few minutes) into Reap in order to use the Reap product suite. You can find Reap Treasury from the sidebar of your Reap Dashboard. Feature overview Reap Treasury allows you to: Get a comprehensive overview of all your funds sent to Reap with detailed breakdown Send funds to Reap for specific uses with ease Reap Card (as collateral to define Reap Card's credit limit) Reap Pay General Move stablecoins between Reap Pay, Reap Card and General balances Supported networks You can transfer digital currencies to Reap for the following networks: Ethereum (ERC20) - USDC & USDT Polygon PoS (ERC20) - USDC & USDT Solana (SPL) - USDC & USDT Tron (TRC20) - USDT Learn more about which assets are supported here . For each transfer of digital currency to Reap for various purposes, Reap will display the equivalent value in fiat currency (HKD or USD). Once our system reconciles your transfer, you will be able to view the updated balance on the Reap Treasury page. How will the different funds be used? Reap Card All the digital currencies sent to your 'Reap Card' balance serve as collateral. They are used to define your credit limit in a 1:1 ratio. Click here to learn how to increase your credit limit using digital currencies. Pro Tips: When making a repayment via stablecoin collateral, your credit limit will be decreased. Reap Pay All digital currencies sent to your 'Reap Pay' balance will be used to facilitate future Reap Pay payments. What is “General”? The General fund has the purpose to allow the user to send funds in Reap without being used right away for a particular designated purpose. If you send funds for your 'Reap Card' balance, they can be used as credit line instantly. If you send funds to the 'Reap Pay' balance, they will be used to fund awaiting Reap Pay payments. However, once funds are deposited in General, they can be moved to different balances for different purposes later. We have plans to extend the use of the General balance in the future to facilitate moving funds between entities, for example.

title: Merge Card Shipments
body: If your team is ordering multiple cards, you can request to have them shipped together in one package. To do this, make sure all card orders use the same contact information. How to Merge Card Orders Into One Shipment To combine multiple card orders into a single shipment, all cards must share the same contact person name, phone number, and address . This applies whether the cards are ordered by an admin or by individual team members. If the Admin Is Ordering for the Team Use the same contact name, phone number, and shipping address for every card order. This ensures we can group them into one shipment. If Team Members Are Ordering Their Own Cards Ask all team members to enter the same contact name, phone number, and shipping address during the ordering process. Matching contact details will help us merge the shipments. 💡 Card orders with different contact details will be shipped separately. Need Help? If you're unsure or need help with combining card shipments, Know who to talk to @ Reap We're here to help ensure smooth and efficient delivery for your team.

title: How To Apply For Reap Card
body: You can apply for Reap Card by logging into your Reap Dashboard and completing the application form. This guide walks you through each step of the process. 1. Start Your Application Log into your Reap Dashboard Click Reap Card in the left-hand menu Select Apply Now to begin 2. Enter Company Details Provide your company name, business type, and a short description of what your business does. Include a website or social media link if available If none is available, describe your business activities, product/service offerings, target customers, sales channels, and estimated monthly revenue 3. Add Business Address and Registration Info Enter your company’s registered business address and business registration number. 4. Submit Financial Information You can share your company’s financial info in two ways: Connect your bank account (fastest) Authorise a one-time link using Finverse It takes about 5 minutes to connect You'll see "Linked Successful" when the connection is complete Link all company accounts if you use more than one Upload bank documents Choose this if your bank isn’t supported or you prefer not to link your account Learn more about Finverse and how we secure your data 5. Add Business Owner Details For each business owner: Provide legal name and date of birth as shown on their HKID Add all business owners to this section Need help identifying your company’s Ultimate Beneficial Owners? Learn more 6. Verify Your Phone Number Complete verification via WhatsApp, SMS, or phone call. 7. Review and Submit Review all your application details to ensure they’re accurate. Once ready, click Submit . You’ll receive a confirmation email once we’ve received your application. Need Help? If you have any questions during the process, you can schedule a demo or contact our team for assistance.

title: Understanding Ultimate Beneficial Ownership
body: When you open a Reap account for a company or other legal entity, we’re required to collect information about its ultimate beneficial owner (UBO) . This helps ensure transparency and meet international financial regulations. Who Counts as an Ultimate Beneficial Owner An ultimate beneficial owner is any individual who: Owns or controls, directly or indirectly, more than 25% of the legal entity, or Otherwise has the right or ability to exercise ultimate control over the entity This includes individuals who may not hold shares but still have significant influence over decisions—like executive officers or senior managers (e.g. CEO, CFO, Managing Partner, Board Chair, etc.). 💡 If ownership is split among related parties (such as spouses or dependents), their combined influence may also meet the 25% threshold. If No One Owns 25% or More If no individual directly or indirectly owns 25% or more, you’ll need to identify the person who exercises ultimate control over your business. This is typically someone in a senior management or executive role. If Your Company Is Owned by Another Company You still need to identify the individual who ultimately controls your legal entity—even if it’s owned by another company. This means tracing ownership through any parent companies until a person with more than 25% control is identified. For example: If you own 60% of Company A, and Company A owns 50% of Company B, you effectively control 30% of Company B and would be considered its beneficial owner. What Information You’ll Need to Provide For each ultimate beneficial owner, we’ll ask for: Full legal name A copy of their HKID (for Hong Kong residents) or passport (for non-residents) When We’ll Ask for This We’ll request beneficial owner details: When a new Reap account is opened on behalf of a company or other legal entity If you're the primary user of an existing account and this information hasn’t yet been provided Why This Is Required Collecting UBO information is part of global efforts to prevent financial crime. Legal entities can be misused for activities like money laundering, terrorism financing, tax evasion, and fraud. Verifying who ultimately controls a company helps protect both your business and the wider financial system. Need Help? If you’re unsure who qualifies as a beneficial owner or how to proceed, Know who to talk to @ Reap for guidance. We’re here to help.

title: Guide to Corporate Documents
body: General Guide Proof of ID Valid passport with clear photo and signature National identification card issued by a recognized government authority Driver's license with clear photo and signature Proof of Address Bank statement or Government-issued documents showing the residential address issued within the last three months Proof of Business Address Lease agreement or Utility bill for the business premises issued within the last three months Municipal tax assessment or government-issued document indicating the business address Certificate of Incumbency Recent certificate of incumbency issued by a competent authority Document issued by the Companies Registry or similar government agency Issued within 6 months Register of Directors (ROD) Certified or notarized copy of the register of directors obtained from the Companies Registry Official document issued by a competent authority indicating the current directors Register of Members (ROM) Certified or notarized copy of the register of members obtained from the Companies Registry Official document issued by a competent authority indicating the current members/shareholders Name change certificate Legal document confirming the change of name issued by a competent authority For Individuals Marriage certificate or divorce decree indicating the change of name Must provide together with identity documents with old name and new name Business Registration Valid and up-to-date business registration certificate issued by the relevant government authority Incorporation certificate or similar official document indicating the legal existence of the business Organizational Chart Clearly labelled and detailed organizational chart of the company structure. Dated and signed by director(s) Company Reports Guide to corporate documents (Hong Kong) When you sign up for a Reap account, you’ll need to submit the following: Companies (incorporated in Hong Kong) Company name; and Business Registration Certificate number. This information can be found on the Business Registration Certificate issued by the Inland Revenue Department, as shown by the red circles in the example below: Directors, Ultimate Beneficial Owners, Account Users Legal name; Date of birth; and HKID copy (Hong Kong residents); or NRIC copy (Singapore citizens); or Passport copy (non-Hong Kong residents/Singapore citizens). When submitting a passport copy (i.e. for non-Hong Kong residents/Singapore citizens), please provide a copy of the passport’s biometric page (i.e. the page with your photo and personal details). What other documents may Reap ask for? If we need further information, our risk/compliance team may ask you to provide additional documents during the onboarding process. These may include: Business Registration Certificate : issued by the Inland Revenue Department, this document is required for conducting business in Hong Kong. You can obtain an electronic extract of your company’s Business Registration Certificate here . Certificate of Incorporation : issued by the Companies Registry, this document is issued to all limited liability companies incorporated in Hong Kong. Annual Return : submitted to the Companies Registry, this document contains all the particulars of your company, including registered office address, names of shareholders, directors, company secretary, etc. Shareholding chart : a chart showing the percentage ownership and the percentage of voting rights held by the shareholders of your company. In some instances we may need the chart to be signed by a director or verified by a CPA/solicitor of Hong Kong that it is accurate and valid. Articles of Association : along with the memorandum of association (for companies incorporated before 3 March 2014 only), this document forms the constitution of your company, setting out matters such as director’s responsibilities, constitution and powers of the board of directors, issuance/transfer of shares, and shareholder’s rights. Financial documents : these include- Bank statements (i.e. within last 2 months); Financial statements (i.e. audit reports, typically produced annually); and Corporate credit card statement (i.e. within last 2 months). Additional supporting documents : these include sales contracts, invoices, and customer lists (top 10 only), mainly for us to have more context of your company’s line of business (i.e. which products/services your company offers).

title: Guide to ID Verification
body: Who are required to submit IDs upon account opening? Significant controller (e.g. Ultimate Beneficial Owners, or UBOs) of the account-opening entity. Read more about beneficial owners and significant controllers here . Reap Account Representative/ PPTA (person purporting to act on behalf of the client or entity) - The authorised person in charge of managing the Reap account. This person can be your Finance Manager, HR Manager, or Admin Personnel and does not necessarily have to be a Director or a UBO of the account-opening entity. What type of IDs are accepted? A valid (non-expired), government-issued passport with the bearer’s photo. A valid (non-expired), government-issued ID (front and back) with the bearer’s photo. What forms of the ID or passport copies are accepted? Reap prefers a photo of the original copy of the IDs or passports, in JPG/JPEG/PNG format only. Alternatively, you can upload a Certified True Copy (CTC) of the passport or government ID of your UBOs and your Business Representative (Reap account representative). How to submit ID or Passport during Reap account opening? Choose the Passport/ID issuing country/region. Take a photo ( preferably on mobile ) or upload a photo of the relevant person’s ID or passport from your device (desktop or mobile). On Desktop: On Mobile: Important points to note before uploading or taking a photo of the IDs or passports: Use a photo of the original copy of the IDs or passports, not a scanned copy or photocopy. Take a clear photo of your ID or passport with all four corners visible. Ensure no watermarks are present. Do not edit the picture (e.g., adjust the colour) as it could be detected as digital tampering. The photo must be in colour; black and white photos are not acceptable. All information must be readable in the photo. Ensure there are no glares in the ID or passport photo. Photos of the original ID or passport are preferably in JPEG format. What happens if the submitted ID verification fails? If you are prompted to resubmit again during the application, do ensure your ID or passport photo meets all the above-mentioned specified requirements before re-uploading the photo. Our Onboarding or Compliance team will reach out to you and provide a new link for you to re-upload a specific individual’s ID or passport if the review team finds out that your ID or passport photo did not meet our requirements after you had submitted your application. Alternatively, you can send over a Certified True Copy (CTC) of the passport or government ID of your UBOs and your Business Representative (Reap account representative) to us via email at support@reapglobal.co , indicating your entity’s full legal name and account representative’s email.

title: What Is Reap?
body: Reap is a financial platform that helps businesses manage payments, expenses, and card programs in one place. Our tools are designed to support companies of all sizes — giving teams more control, visibility, and flexibility over how they spend and operate. What You Can Do With Reap Issue and manage cards through Reap’s secure card-issuing API Track and control expenses using our Visa-backed Reap Card and expense management software Send and receive payments globally with Reap Pay Reap Card Reap Card is a corporate credit card powered by Visa. It helps teams manage spending with built-in features like real-time alerts, expense tracking, and custom spending limits. Reap Pay Reap Pay is a global payment solution for sending and receiving business payments. It supports payouts to suppliers, vendors, and employees using various methods — all in one platform.

title: Best Practices on How to Increase Reap Card Available Balance
body: You can increase your Reap Card’s available balance by either depositing more collateral or repaying your monthly balance. Here’s how each method works and what to expect based on your billing cycle. Summary Repay your monthly balance to restore your available balance Deposit more collateral to increase your credit capacity Late repayments may result in interest charges What Happens During the Billing Cycle Let’s say you deposited $1,000 USDC as collateral and spent $500 USD by January 31st. If You Repay Before February 20th Repaying $500 by February 20th will restore your available balance to $1,000. The increase takes effect as soon as the repayment is processed. If You Don’t Repay by February 21st If no repayment is made, your available balance drops to $500 starting February 21st. Interest may apply from this date until repayment is made. 💡 Late repayments may incur an incremental interest fee in the following billing cycle. Increase Balance by Depositing More Collateral You can add more collateral at any time using the Increase Credit via Deposit option. This immediately increases your available balance. Depositing more does not count as a repayment. You’ll still need to repay any unpaid balance separately to avoid interest charges. Increase Balance by Making a Repayment After receiving your monthly statement, you can use the Repayment option to pay off your balance. Once repaid, your available balance increases by the same amount. You can only repay up to the billed amount—your available balance can’t exceed your collateral. Related Article: How does the Reap Card billing cycle work? Need Help? If you’re unsure how much to repay or when to deposit, visit Know who to talk to @ Reap for support.

title: Handling a Departing Employee's Reap Card
body: When an employee leaves your company, it’s important to secure their Reap account and card access. Follow these steps to freeze their card, back up their transaction history, and remove their profile if needed. Summary Freeze the card immediately to prevent any use Back up all transactions before removing the employee Contact Reap if they are a Business Representative or Owner Freeze the Card Immediately Log into the Reap Dashboard and go to the card details. Select Edit Card and choose Freeze to immediately stop any further transactions. 💡 Freezing the card does not delete the employee’s profile—it only disables the card. Back Up Transactions Before deleting the team member’s profile, make sure you’ve done either of the following: Sync their transactions to your accounting integration, or Export their full transaction history as a backup Check If the Employee's Profile Can Be Deleted In the Dashboard, go to the Team tab and locate the employee. You can delete them if they are not listed as a Business Representative or Business Owner . Contact Us If They Are a Representative or Owner If the employee is a Business Representative or Owner, contact us before they leave. Email support@reapglobal.co so our Customer Success team can help with the necessary checks before securely removing their profile. Need help? For any questions or additional help, see Know who to talk to @ Reap . We're here to support you.

title: Updated ATM Withdrawal Limits and Country Restrictions
body: Starting 1 December 2025, new limits and restrictions will apply to ATM withdrawals. These updates are part of our ongoing efforts to improve security and comply with global regulatory standards. Summary New daily, monthly, and yearly ATM withdrawal limits will apply ATM withdrawals will be restricted in certain high-risk jurisdictions Changes take effect at 8:00 AM HKT on 1 December 2025 New ATM Withdrawal Limits ATM withdrawal limits will now differ based on your card currency. These limits apply to all cardholders: USD-Based Cards Daily Limit: USD 3,000 Monthly Limit: USD 10,000 Yearly Limit: USD 100,000 Daily Frequency: Up to 3 transactions Monthly Frequency: Up to 30 transactions Yearly Frequency: No limit HKD-Based Cards Daily Limit: HKD 25,000 Monthly Limit: HKD 80,000 Yearly Limit: HKD 800,000 Daily Frequency: Up to 3 transactions Monthly Frequency: Up to 30 transactions Yearly Frequency: No limit 💡 These limits are automatically applied and do not require any action from you. Geo Restrictions for ATM Withdrawals ATM withdrawals will be blocked in the following locations: China – Restricted due to increased risk of ATM-related fraud FATF Blacklisted Countries – Always restricted OFAC-Sanctioned Countries – Always restricted These restrictions are in place to reduce fraud and protect your account. Why These Changes Are Being Made These updates are designed to: Reduce exposure to fraud and money-laundering risks Limit ATM access in high-risk or sanctioned regions Strengthen transaction monitoring, especially for cash activities Need Help? If you have questions or need support, Know who to talk to @ Reap for the best point of contact. Our team is ready to help.

Step 3: Enter your credentials on the accounting software login page then Allow access. After the connection is completed, you will be able to see the status on Reap dashboard changed to ✓ Connected. On the accounting software, you will be able to find Reap Card as one of the connected accounts on the main menu. Take a tour

title: How to enable two-factor authentication (2FA) for my Reap account?
body: 2FA can add an additional layer of security to your account, and protect you from phishing and attacks. To enable 2FA for your Reap account, simply follow the steps below: Login to Reap dashboard and click on the account icon in the upper right corner. Go to Account settings . Go to Two-step authentication and click on Add authentication step . You can choose to receive the authentication code via Authenticator, Text message or WhatsApp . Pick an authentication method of your choice then click Continue . On the next login attempt, our system will prompt you to provide the authentication code in order to login into your account.

title: How to Connect Reap Card to Google Wallet
body: This article explains how you can add and remove your Reap cards to your mobile device with Google Pay. Adding your Card With Reap Mobile App Download the Reap app from Google Play Store Open the Reap app and Login to your account. Click the "Add to Google Wallet" button at the bottom of the card panel Read the Issuer Terms and tap Accept. If you’re asked to verify your payment method, choose an option from the list. Learn how to verify your payment method. With the Google Wallet App Open the Google Wallet app . At the bottom, tap Add to Wallet. Tap Payment Card . Any cards you saved to your Google Account are shown. Tap New credit or debit card . To add a card, use your phone camera to scan the Reap Card details on the dashboard, or tap Enter details manually . At the bottom, tap Save . Read the Issuer Terms and tap Accept . If you’re asked to verify your payment method, choose an option from the list. Learn how to verify your payment method. After the addition, you should get a message that your card was added and is ready to make contactless payments in stores, online, or in apps where Google Pay is accepted. After you add a card, you might find a small transaction on your account from Google Wallet. This transaction checks that your card and account are valid. This transaction will soon disappear and doesn’t affect your balance. Removing your card With the Google Wallet app Open your Google Wallet app Click on the card that you want to remove Tap the three dots in the top right corner Tap “Remove payment method” Follow the prompts to finalize the removal of your card from your Google Wallet

title: Can I Move funds between the different Reap balances?
body: In Reap Treasury, you can move your stablecoins between the different Reap balances if you want to consolidate them. You can move funds between Reap Card, Reap Pay and General. Please note that you cannot move funds from your Reap Card collateral into Reap Pay or General. From / To Reap Card Reap Pay General Reap Pay Yes Yes Yes General Yes Yes Yes Reap Card No No No What happens when moving funds? When moving funds to Reap Card: Your Card collateral will increase by the transferred amount When moving funds to Reap Pay: Any payments awaiting funds will be processed if the amount transferred covers the amount to be paid out. When moving funds to General: No effect How to Navigate to Reap Treasury and find the Move Funds button at the top right of the screen In Move funds, follow the flow by selecting the origin and designation of funds and press Transfer funds to complete the transfer. Reminder: transfers into your Card collateral are irreversible! You can contact us directly if you need support at support@reapglobal.co . The funds should be credited within a minute!

title: Reap Card ATM Withdrawal Limits
body: To help protect your account and stay compliant with global financial regulations, Reap Card has daily, monthly, and yearly limits on ATM withdrawals. This article outlines how these limits work and what to expect if you reach them. Summary Daily and monthly withdrawal amounts and frequencies are capped There are no yearly withdrawal limits ATM withdrawals are not supported in certain high-risk countries Daily ATM Withdrawal Limit You can withdraw up to USD 3,000 or HKD 25,000 per day, with a maximum of 3 ATM withdrawals per day . Monthly ATM Withdrawal Limit Each company can withdraw up to USD 10,000 or HKD 80,000 per month, with a maximum of 30 ATM withdrawals per month . Yearly ATM Withdrawal Limit There is no annual limit on withdrawal amount or frequency. ATM Withdrawals Not Available in Certain Countries To stay aligned with Financial Action Task Force (FATF) guidance, ATM withdrawals are not supported in these countries: Democratic People's Republic of Korea (North Korea) Iran Myanmar If your business operates from or transacts through these countries, ATM withdrawals will be disabled. Frequently Asked Questions Why are there withdrawal limits? These limits help reduce risk, prevent fraud, and ensure compliance with financial regulations. What happens if I reach my withdrawal limit? If you exceed your daily or monthly limit, any additional withdrawal attempts will be declined. You'll need to wait until the next day or month, depending on the limit reached. Can I request higher withdrawal limits? Yes. To request a change, contact our customer success team with: The new amount and frequency you’d like A brief explanation of your intended use for the cash withdrawal How can I track my ATM withdrawals? You can view your ATM withdrawal history in the Transactions section of your Reap dashboard under Reap Card. Need Help? If you have any other questions or need help with ATM withdrawals, please reach out to our team. You can always refer to Know who to talk to @ Reap for the best point of contact.

title: Getting Started With Reap Pay
body: Once your business verification is complete, you can start using Reap Pay to make payments. This guide walks you through the basic setup to send your first payment and manage your team settings. Choose a Funding Method You can fund your Reap Pay payments in two ways: using your Reap Card or by sending funds to your Reap Pay balance. Use Your Reap Card To pay with your Reap Card, make sure it’s already set up in your account. Only Reap-issued cards are supported—non-Reap cards aren't accepted. Use Your Reap Pay Balance If you'd rather fund payments from your Reap Pay balance, first transfer funds to your Reap Pay wallet. You can do this using supported stablecoins. Create Your First Payment Once your funding source is ready, you can create a Reap Pay payment. Watch a quick product tour Optional: Invite Team Members or Set Up Approvals If you're working with a team, you can give others access or build in payment checks. Invite Team Members Easily add colleagues so they can manage or view payments with you. Set Up an Approval Policy Create approval flows to match your internal payment controls. Need Help? If you have questions or need help getting started, we’re here to support you. Know who to talk to @ Reap

title: How to create your first payment on Reap
body: Reap Pay can help you effectively utilize your Reap Card/ or crypto funds to settle expenses that traditionally do not accept credit card payments. To use Reap Pay to make a payment, you need to provide the below information: Once you have logged into your Reap account, click on "Reap Pay" on the left hand side of the web page, then click on "Payments" Click on "+ Add payment" button on the top right hand corner of the page. Then click on "+ Add a new recipient" button to set the recipient currency, category and type Next, choose whether to make the payment via bank transfer or FPS transfer. Then input the corresponding account information required. Provide payment details, such as specifying how much the recipient gets; the payment reference; how frequently the payment should repeat; and when the payment should arrive. You will be required to upload an invoice or supporting document After filling out the payment details, click "Continue" and input your credit card information Once you finish filling in all the required information, you can review and confirm all the information you have entered. You will also need to agree to the terms and conditions before submitting your payment

title: Why my Reap Card transactions were declined?
body: We all understand the pain of card transaction decline. We are here to provide some tips for you to work on some initial troubleshooting. First, please make sure you have activated your card, understand if there are any spending restrictions applied on the card by your company admin, and confirm the card is not frozen. Next, check if you have received any email notifications from Reap regarding the transaction. If you have received the email, we will mention the reason in the message. Common decline messages: Insufficient fund: Your Reap Card total or sub-card balance is insufficient to pay the transaction. Please visit the dashboard to confirm your card balance before making a large-volume transaction. EH timeout: The payment processing time is longer than expected. Please retry the transaction 15 minutes later. Category restrictions: Please contact your Group Owner and/or admin for details, and see if they can lift the restrictions for you to finish the transaction. Also, you can view all declined transactions on Reap Card → Transactions → Declined tab. Troubleshooting failed transactions without email notifications There are situations where your vendor has rejected the card transaction before the request is sent to our side. As a result, there are some declined transactions that you do not get to receive email notifications or view the record on your dashboard. Please try some of these methods and see if they help you proceed further. Removing Autofill Some browsers allow you to save your credit card information and click to fill in the details when you check out your shopping card. However, please note that some vendors may reject transactions if the card number is auto-filled. Please try to type the card number and details whenever possible. VPN Connection If your device is connected to a Virtual Private Network (VPN), there is a chance that the transaction cannot be completed. Please try turning it off and retrying the transaction. Need more help? If you tried the ways above and are still unable to complete a Reap Card transaction, feel free to send us a message via live chat or email: support@reapglobal.co Please include the following information for a better understanding of your situation. Vendor of the transaction Time of attempting the transaction Steps that you have completed or stuck (e.g. I entered the SMS OTP, but the transaction page went blank and no order is confirmed)

title: Connecting My Reap Account to Accounting Integrations: Step-by-step Guide
body: Step 1: Connect Reap Account to Accounting Integrations Related articles: ​ How to connect Reap Card to accounting software? | Reap Help Center How do I disconnect from accounting integrations? | Reap Help Center Step 2: Map Reap Card expenses and Spending Category Please complete this step if you wish to sync your transactions to the accounting integrations. Setting up transaction mapping rules will save you time when syncing your transactions to accounting software. Related article: How to map Reap Card expenses to Accounting Software | Reap Help Center Step 3: Set up ‘Groups’ in Reap and match them with the spend category in Accounting Software Please complete this step if you wish to sync all transactions as reconciled transactions in accounting software. Reap’s Groups feature organises your team members into ‘Groups’ (e.g. by department or location), which can correspond to your tracking categories. If you have already set up ‘Groups’ in Reap, you may skip this step. Related article: ​ What is a Group and how to make updates? | Reap Help Center Set up tracking categories Step 4: Review and Sync Reap transactions to Accounting Software There are two ways to sync all your Reap transactions to accounting software. You can pick the option that best suits your accounting practice. To provide more flexibility for you and your team, you may use different methods for individual line items. We use Xero's integration as one of the examples. Related article: How to map Reap Card expenses to Accounting Software | Reap Help Center Option 1: Sync as reconciled (shown in the ‘Account Transactions’ section under the ‘Accounting’ tab in Xero) Directly sync to the ‘Account Transaction’ section as a ‘reconciled’ transition with details like Xero. Reap’s ‘Groups’ is equivalent to the spend category in Xero. Mapping categories allows transactions to automatically select the correct chart of accounts and reconcile without redundant work, saving hours on your month-end close. Related article: How to sync Reap Card transactions to Xero | Reap Help Center Option 2: Sync as a bank statement (Shown in the ‘Bank Statements and Reconcile’ section under the ‘Accounting’ tab in Xero) Pull all transactions into the ‘Bank Statement’ tab as ‘unreconciled’ for you to process further in Xero You can do the reconciliation process in Xero Related article: How do I reconcile data synced to Accounting Integrations? | Reap Help Center 💡 You can bulk-select the transactions and synchronise all transactions in one go 💡 Syncing transactions manually will give you more flexibility in choosing which sync methods to use Related article: When and how does data sync occur? | Reap Help Center Related Article: Accounting Integration FAQ | Reap Help Center

title: How to add a new entity to your Organization
body: Reap allows you to have multiple entities onboarded under the same organisation. Making navigation and access a lot easier than having multiple logins. Terminology Entity : we define an entity as a separate business. Meaning a different Reap Account. Organisation : an organisation is a group of entities. An entity can only belong to one Organisation. What are the benefits? This allows you to be able to use the same email address and login across multiple entities onboarded with Reap. You can add multiple entities under the same Organisation to be able to easily switch between them. What are the limitations? Your different entities are still different Reap accounts, which means all funds, transactions and settings are not shared. Only Admins of the Reap account will be able to add new entities What are the different use cases of using multiple entities? You have different legal entities that need to be onboarded in Reap You want to open an account in another currency for Reap Card and Reap Pay for the same entity (you will need to onboard the same entity again under a different currency) You want to extend your usage of Reap products. For example, on top of your Reap Card and Pay, you wish to start your own card program. To use the Card Issuance API or the Pay API, contact us at support@reapglobal.co . How to add a new entity? To add a new entity, open the entity switcher at the top of the sidebar and click "Add Entity to Organisation" You will see a prompt appear where you can enter your new entity name and select a Reap Product. You can choose between: Reap Card and Pay : Create a new account to have access to Reap Card and Reap Pay Card Issuance API : Build your own card program and issue branded Visa cards to your clients. Learn more here. Pay API : Create payments via API. Contact us at support@reapglobal.co for more information CAUTION: You will not be able to choose the product type after creating the entity. Contact us at support@reapglobal.co for more info. You will then be redirected to the flow to complete your account verification (KYB) for that new entity. Once completed, our team will review your application. You can now already switch to your new entity from the entity switcher What if I already have another Reap account? Our team can support you in merging multiple Reap account that you own under the same email and the same organisation. Contact us a support@reapglobal.co if you want us to support you with this process. Our team will reserve the right to ask for verification documents to ensure you are part of the two companies. Can I add team members from other entities? Yes, you can use the same flow as a team invite to add team members into entities. If they are members of the same organisations, the flow will be even simpler. Read more here: How to Add Team Members to Different Entities Can I switch entity from the mobile app? Yes, once you have access to multiple entities, you can also switch from the mobile app to access all your cards. Navigate to settings and use the Switch Business option to switch.

title: In-App One-time Password (OTP) Card Transaction Authentication
body: Reap Card is a 3D Secure card with an additional layer of security to protect against fraud and unauthorised access. Therefore, some online merchants may require additional verification to ensure that it's an authorised transaction. One of the most common ways to verify a transaction is using an SMS one-time password. However, this process can be time-consuming and inconvenient, especially if you're in an area with poor network connectivity. But with this in-app transaction authentication feature, you can now choose to verify the transaction within the Reap app. This feature provides a fast, secure, and hassle-free way to authorise your transactions. How to activate the In-App Transaction Authentication? Install Reap App and log in with your Reap account The Reap App is available for both Android and Apple users. Simply click on the links below to download the app: Android user: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.reaphq.reapmobile Apple user: https://apps.apple.com/hk/app/reap-global/id1660845868?l=en Make a Transaction : When you make an online transaction using your Reap Card, you may be prompted to authorise it. Choose Reap App as the verification method : you will only see the second option if your device has the app installed. Log into Your Reap App : Simply log in to your Reap App using your Reap dashboard credentials or biometric data, such as your fingerprint or face recognition. Approve the Transaction : Once you're logged in, you will see the details of the transaction that needs approval. Check the details and approve the transaction with a simple tap. What to Do If You Don't Receive the SMS OTP During Online Purchases If you are unable receive the SMS OTP during online purchases, simply download and log in to your Reap App to authorise the transaction using In-App Transaction Authentication instead. This feature ensures that you can authorise your transactions quickly and securely, without the need for SMS OTPs.

title: Reap Card Pricing and Fees
body: Fee Type Cost Reap Card is a secured corporate credit card with no annual or hidden fees. This article outlines what fees to expect, how repayments work, and important terms related to card usage. Summary No annual or hidden service fees Monthly repayment required Multiple repayment options with no fee No Annual Fees or Hidden Charges Reap Card does not charge an annual fee. All core services, including card issuance, replacement, and cancellation, are free. Monthly Repayment Required As a secured credit card, Reap Card requires you to repay your outstanding balance monthly. You’ll receive an invoice at the end of each statement period. How to Repay Your Balance You can repay using one of two methods: Fiat payment : No fees apply. Deducting from stablecoin collateral : Also free, but reduces your available credit limit. This action is not automated by default. You can choose your preferred repayment method each month. Reap Visa Card Fee Schedule Fee Type Cost Annual Fee Free Card Transaction (domestic) Free Card Transaction (international) 2% and only if the foreign transaction was made in a currency other than your Domestic Currency (1) ATM Withdrawal 2% of the transaction amount Transaction Refund Free Chargeback Dispute Handling Fee Free Card Cancellation Free Card Replacement Free Minimum Payment The Minimum Payment is the minimum amount you need to pay for an Account Statement in your Domestic Currency. For HKD Cards: - If the Account Statement balance is HK$2,000 or more: 1% of your total outstanding balance, or HK$2,000 (whichever is higher) - If the Account Statement balance is less than HK$2,000: the statement balance For USD Cards - If the Account Statement balance is US$250 or more: 1% of your total outstanding balance, or US$250 (whichever is higher) - If the Account Statement balance is less than US$250: the statement balance Late Payment Fee HK$230 or Minimum Payment (whichever is higher) for HKD Cards US$30 or Minimum Payment (whichever is higher) for USD Cards Return Check / Reject Autopay Fee Free Repayment Fee Repayment with fiat - Free Repayment with collateral in stablecoins - Free Interest Rates and Finance Charges Description Annualised Percentage Rate (APR) (2) for overdue balance on Card purchases 25.00% APR. We will not charge you a finance charge if you pay your Account Statement in full by the due date each month, otherwise, a finance charge will be charged on the unpaid statement balance from the date of the previous statement on a daily basis until payment in full. Interest-Free Period Up to 51 days, meaning 20 days after each statement issue date. Unlike most credit card issuers, at Reap, this interest-free period on the previous Account Statement’s spending continues to be available to you even if you have an overdue balance. Minimum Payment Due If we do not receive the Minimum Payment for an account statement, the Late Payment Fee will apply to that account statement. Notwithstanding the above and subject to the Interest-Free Period, interest is charged on any outstanding statement balance. (1) Domestic Currency is the settlement currency of your Card, which is Hong Kong Dollars or US dollars, depending on your choice of Card. (2) APR is calculated according to the Net Present Value Method as specified in the Code of Banking Practice. The Finance Charge will be calculated at the applicable rate on a 365-day yearly basis.

title: How do I reconcile data synced to accounting integrations?
body: After synchronising Reap Card transactions to the accounting integrations, you might want to reconcile the data there. You can click on Sync to Integrations , then select Sync as reconciled . On the accounting integration, click on your Reap Card account to view the transactions. On the Account transactions tab, you will see the transactions you have synchronised from Reap dashboard showing the status Unreconciled. If you wish to edit the transaction details before reconciliation, click on Options -> Edit Transaction. You will be able to edit the Description, Account, Transaction Date, and more. When you finish editing, click Update to save and go back to the Account transaction page. If the transaction details are correct, you can reconcile the transaction directly by clicking Mark as Reconciled .

title: Understanding Reap Card Transaction Declines
body: If a Reap Card transaction fails, you can usually find the reason in your dashboard, mobile app, or email. This guide explains where to find decline details and what each decline code means. Where to Find Decline Reasons You can view the decline reason for a failed transaction from these places: Reap Dashboard: Go to Reap Card > Transactions , then click on a transaction to open the details drawer. Reap Mobile App: Tap on any transaction to view its details. Email Notification: If a transaction fails, you may receive an email with the decline reason. If you didn’t receive an email, check your spam folder and make sure your account email is up to date. Reap Card Decline Codes and What They Mean Here are common decline codes and what they indicate: Code Description 3 The merchant is not set up to accept this type of card. Please try using a different payment method. 5 The transaction has been declined due to do not honour. 41 The card has been reported as lost. 46 The card is no longer active. 51 Not enough balance to complete the transaction. Please add funds or use a different payment method. 54 The card has expired. Please use a valid card. 55 The entered PIN is incorrect. Please try again. 57 Something went wrong when processing the transaction. Please contact Reap for support. 58 The transaction is not allowed at this point of sale. 59 Something went wrong when processing the transaction. Please contact Reap for support. 61 You have exceeded your ATM withdrawal limit. Please try a smaller amount or wait for the limit to reset. 62 ATM withdrawals are not allowed in this country for your card. 65 You have exceeded the allowed number of ATM withdrawals. Please try again later. 70 Card status is not normal. Please contact Reap. 75 The card has been blocked due to exceeding the maximum number of incorrect ATM PIN attempts. 77 Reap does accept transactions for this merchant category 82 Transaction took too long to process, please try again. 86 The system was unable to verify your PIN due to a technical issue or incorrect input, so the transaction could not be completed. 91 Issuer Or Switch Is Inoperative. N9 There's a discrepancy between the transaction records of the payment systems involved. N0 An unexpected error happened before the payment could be fully processed. N3 Something went wrong when processing the transaction. Please contact Reap for support. N7 The CVV entered is incorrect. Please check and try again. 6P Verification of the provided data failed. Please verify and try again. R1 The card is frozen. Please contact your account Admin. R2 The card has been blocked due to multiple incorrect CVV attempts. R3 The card has been blocked due to multiple incorrect expiry date attempts. R4 The card is not activated yet. Please activate it from the Reap mobile app or the Reap Dashboard. R5 Withdrawals are not allowed for this card. R6 The daily ceiling allowed to spend for this card has been exceeded. Please contact your Admin to request an increase. R7 The spending frequency allowed for this card has been exceeded. Please contact your Admin. R8 The card is blocked. R9 Not enough balance to complete the transaction for this merchant category. Please add funds. R10 Insufficient funds in the budget associated with this card. Please contact your Admin to increase it. R11 The budget associated with this card has been frozen. Please contact Reap. Why You Might Not Receive a Decline Notification If a transaction failed but you didn’t get a notification: Check your spam folder. Sometimes these emails are filtered. Check your email address. Make sure it's correct in your account settings. Merchant-side issues. Some merchants decline non-local cards before the request reaches Reap. You may need to contact the merchant for more information. Need Help? If you're unsure about a decline reason or need help resolving an issue, know who to talk to at Reap . Our team is here to help.

title: Why Some Users Are Marked as Business Owner or Representative
body: Some users in your Reap account may be labeled as Business Owners or Representatives. These roles help determine who has access to key actions and features, especially those that require identity verification. Summary Certain user roles require identity verification (KYC) Some account actions are limited to specific roles Role information changes must be submitted through support User Roles That Require KYC Some roles require Know Your Customer (KYC) verification based on their level of authority in the business: Business Representative The person who submitted the company’s application. This role may not be labeled directly in the dashboard. Ultimate Beneficial Owner (UBO) Anyone who owns more than 25% of the company’s shares. Significant Controller Anyone who has control over company decisions, even if they don’t own shares. 💡 These roles are defined as part of Reap’s compliance with regulatory standards. Actions Limited to Verified Roles Only verified Business Representatives, UBOs, or Significant Controllers can: Request to increase or decrease the credit limit Request to close the account Where to View Role Information You can view most role labels, like Business Owners and Significant Controllers, on the Teams page of your Reap dashboard. Business Representatives are not labeled directly. If you’re unsure who holds this role, contact our support team for confirmation. Updating Role Information If your business needs to update details (like email, phone number, or name) for any verified role, contact us at support@reapglobal.co . Processing usually takes 5–10 working days. We may also request updated KYC documentation. Need Help? If you’re unsure who holds a role or need help with a request, reach out to Know who to talk to @ Reap . We're here to help.

title: How to track payments with Reap?
body: Payment Tracking provides greater visibility into your payment journeys, allowing you to confirm whether a payment has reached the recipient. What is Payment Tracking? Payment statuses in Reap (e.g., Awaiting Funds, Pending, Completed) indicate the progress of a payment. With Payment Tracking, you can now see updates beyond when a payment is completed, including confirmation of its arrival at the recipient’s bank. How can you track the progress of your payment once its status changes to “Completed”? Reminder : Payment tracking is currently available for SWIFT, CHATS and FPS (Hong Kong) payments from the following currencies (USD, AUD, NZD, CNY, HKD, JPY, SGD, CHF, GBP, and EUR). For payment on rails that are not SWIFT, CHATS, FPS . or that require manual intervention, the dashboard will clearly indicate that the payment does not have tracking capabilities. For details on these payments please contact our team at support@reapglobal.co for assistance. Navigate to your account page on our website. In the left-hand menu, select Reap Pay > Payments . From the table, choose the specific payment you want to track. Note: Ensure the payment status is marked as “Completed.” A pop-up window will appear. Scroll down a bit until you see the Payment Tracking section. From there, you will find a quick overview of the following information: Status: This refers to a set of dynamic payment tracking statuses, which are separate from the original payment statuses (e.g., Compliance Review, Pending, Completed). These tracking statuses can change over time. Identifier: Displays the identifier that is associated with the payment. For SWIFT payments this is the UETR (Unique End-to-End Transaction Reference). Payment Network : Quick indication of which payment network the payment was processed. Document: A downloadable file containing additional details about your payment. Just hit the download button next to the file name and you’ll have a PDF copy of the Proof of Payment. NOTE: This section may take up to 10 minutes to display after the payment reaches a completed status. Payment Tracking Statuses Tracking Status Meaning En route - trackable The payment is on the way and passing through partners with SWIFT GPI tracking capabilities. En route - no longer trackable The payment is in transit but through a non-trackable partner, meaning no further updates will be received. Arrived at recipient The payment has been received and credited by the recipient's bank. Payments table The Tracking Status filter in your payments table allows you to easily sort and review payments based on their tracking status. To apply the filter, navigate to the payments table, locate the Tracking Status dropdown, and select the status you want to filter by. This allows you to focus on specific payments and manage them more efficiently.

title: How to Report Suspected Fraudulent Transactions?
body: Step-by-step Guide Following this guide , please log in to your Reap Dashboard and freeze your Reap Card as soon as possible. Please follow this article to order a new replacement card, if the lost/stolen card is a physical card. If you suspect a fraudulent transaction has been made without your consent, kindly report to our Customer Success Team at support@reapglobal.co from your registered Reap email . Please also refer to the appendix for the request for information. Please provide as much information as you could in the chargeback request. ⚠️ We will only communicate via email regarding all dispute matters to ensure a smooth communication. Please note that the chargeback request must be reported within 70 days . If you have previously provided your card information to a known merchant, the user must show evidence of failure to receive a refund or be unable to contact the merchant in order to raise a chargeback. A chargeback normally takes 3 - 4 months to conclude with a result. If the dispute is won, we will credit the disputed fee back to your card. But if the dispute is lost, your company will be required to settle the payment. Appendix: Information needed to file a Reap Card chargeback/dispute In order to help you resolve the issue as quickly and efficiently as possible, we kindly request that you provide us with the following information and evidence to support your claim: Transaction Details: Transaction ID Transaction date Transaction amount Merchant name Description of the goods or services purchased Cardholder Information: Cardholder registered email Full name of the cardholder Card Details: Card number (last 4 digits) Expiration date Dispute Reason: Please provide a clear and detailed explanation of the reason for the dispute (e.g., unauthorized transaction, billing error, non-receipt of goods/services, etc.). Supporting Documentation: Please provide any evidence supporting the dispute claim (e.g., receipts, invoices, emails, shipping/tracking information, proof of cancellation, etc.). Communication with Merchant: Please provide documentation of attempts to resolve the issue directly with the merchant (e.g., emails, letters, chat logs, etc.). Police Report or Affidavit (if applicable): Please provide a police report or affidavit, if applicable. Timeline of Events: Please provide a clear and chronological timeline of the events leading up to the dispute. Once we receive the required information, our dedicated team will thoroughly review the case. After validating the dispute request from the cardholder, we will initiate the dispute process with Visa promptly.

title: What Is a Secured Credit Card?
body: A secured credit card works like a regular credit card, but it requires an upfront collateral deposit to set your credit limit. The amount you deposit will determine how much you can spend using the card. If you use your collateral to make a repayment, it will reduce your available credit limit. Keep Enough Collateral Available To avoid transaction issues, make sure your account always has enough collateral to support your monthly spending. This helps keep your payments and card usage running smoothly. Repay Your Monthly Balance You’ll need to repay your full balance by the 20th of each month. This includes all transactions from the previous billing cycle. Want to understand how your billing cycle works? Learn more here. We accept repayments in multiple currencies and payment methods. Check our repayment guide for the full list of options. When Collateral Will Be Used We only touch your collateral in two cases: You close your account or reduce your limit We’ll refund your remaining collateral once your balance is fully paid. You miss a repayment If payment isn’t made, we may use your collateral to cover the outstanding balance. Need Help? If you have questions or need support, know who to talk to at Reap . We’re here to help.

Before You Get Started Before sending an invitation, please make sure: You’re inviting the correct person You’re using their business email address (for example, name@company.com ) Personal email domains (such as Gmail, Yahoo, or Outlook) can’t be used for inviting new team members. 💡 If your own Reap account was created using a personal email address, no changes are needed. This requirement applies only to new team invitations. How To Invite a Team Member 1. Log in to your Reap dashboard 2. Go to Team Settings 3. Select Invite Team Member 4. Enter your teammate’s business email address 5. Assign the appropriate role and permissions 6. Send the invitation Your teammate will receive an email with instructions to join your team. Understanding Roles and Permissions When inviting a team member, you’ll need to assign them a role. Each role comes with default permissions, which you can customize further (except for View Only users). Admin Admins have the highest level of access. They can: Add physical or virtual cards Invite new team members View all transactions You can toggle their ability to: Create or edit cards Make Reap Pay payments Manage sub-budgets Group Owner Group Owners are best for department heads or regional managers. They can: Create and assign credit cards within their group Review their group’s transactions You can toggle their ability to: Create or edit cards Make Reap Pay payments Team Member Team Members can: Use their assigned credit card View their own card balance and transactions You can toggle their ability to: Create or edit cards Make Reap Pay payments View Only View Only users can: Review all team members' cards and transactions Approve transactions Handle credit card repayments They cannot : Own a credit card Have permissions customized For more details, see: Compare user-level permission - Reap Card ​ Compare user-level permission - Reap Pay If Your Team Member Can’t Accept the Invite If a team member is unable to accept an invitation: Confirm the invite was sent to their business email address Ask them to use the original invitation email (not a forwarded copy) If you're already using a business email and the issue continues, please contact Reap Support and include: The email domain you are using Whether the issue happened when sending or accepting the invite Any error message you encountered Need Help? title: Understanding the new Reap Pay Balance movements Exports
body: Heads up: this update only applies to businesses that signed up before July 1st, 2025. We’ve made an upgrade to how balance movements are tracked and exported in Reap Pay. This means you’ll now see more detailed and accurate data when you export your transaction history — including entries that were previously hidden, like refunds and manual adjustments . The goal? To give you better transparency across all account movements so it’s easier to reconcile your balances — especially if you're working with a finance team or external accountants. Where to find your balance export You can download your Balance Movements CSV from the Reap Dashboard: Head to Payments or Reap Treasury In the Reap Pay card, click “View history” next to “Total funds” Select “Balance movements” There, you’ll now find two separate CSV exports : Legacy Data — covers all entries before June 30th, 2025 (UTC) New Data — covers all entries from July 1st, 2025 (UTC) onwards What’s different between Legacy and New data? Legacy Data (before July 1st, 2025) Refunds were not shown — refunded payments were removed entirely Adjustments appeared as generic deposits or withdrawals with no context New Data (starting July 1st, 2025) Refunds now show up clearly as a credit with type “Refund” Adjustments now appear as their own entries with type “Adjustment”, and can be credit or debit depending on the action These improvements make it easier to trace every movement and match what’s in your dashboard with what actually happened behind the scenes. What do the new balance movement types mean? Here’s a quick cheat sheet for the different entry types in the new export: Withdrawal – Funds withdrawn at your request ( Debit ) Deposit – Funds added to your account ( Credit ) Refund – A previously funded payment that has been refunded ( Credit ) Payment – A payment made using your Reap Pay balance ( Debit ) Adjustment – A manual balance change made by the Reap team ( Debit or Credit ) Need help understanding your data? If you’re not sure which export to use, our team’s happy to walk you through it. Just reach out via chat or email and we’ll take a look with you.

title: Restricted MCC Codes for Reap Cards
body: Certain merchant categories are blocked from Reap Card usage due to security and compliance requirements. MCCs (Merchant Category Codes) are four-digit codes assigned by credit card networks to classify businesses by type. These codes help manage risk, rewards, and processing rules. Summary Reap Cards block a small number of MCCs for compliance or risk reasons These categories include cash-equivalent transactions, gambling, and money transfers What Is a Merchant Category Code (MCC)? Merchant Category Codes (MCCs) identify the type of goods or services a business provides. Credit card networks use MCCs to: Monitor for fraud or unusual spending Determine eligibility for rewards programs Set processing fees for transactions Each merchant is assigned one MCC when they register with a payment processor. Which MCCs Are Blocked on Reap Cards? Reap Cards will decline any transaction made under the following MCCs: MCC Code Category 4829 Money Transfer 5542 Automated Fuel Dispenser 6010 Financial Institutions – Manual Cash Disbursements 6011 Financial Institutions – ATM/Cash Disbursements 6012 Financial Institutions – Services, Merchandise, and Debt Repayment 6051 Non-Financial Institutions – Foreign Currency, Money Orders, and Debt Repayment 6540 POI Funding Transactions (except MoneySend) 7273 Dating and Escort Services 7995 Gambling and Betting (e.g. casinos, lottery) 9223 Bail and Bond Payments These restrictions apply to all Reap Cardholders. Transactions under these categories will not be approved. Need Help? If you’re unsure why a transaction was declined, or you have questions about specific categories, reach out to our team . We’re here to help.
